title: AI is the New Electricity — Jensen Huang at Davos 2026
executive_summary: Huang argued that AI infrastructure — GPU compute, networking, data centers — is now as fundamental to national and corporate competitiveness as electricity was in the 20th century
Banks and financial institutions that own their AI infrastructure will have a durable cost and capability advantage over those renting cloud AI services
NVIDIA's Blackwell architecture delivers 30x better performance per dollar vs. the previous generation, fundamentally changing the economics of running large language models in-house
Huang predicted that every major financial institution will operate its own AI data center within five years — not for privacy alone, but for inference cost competitiveness
The speech triggered a reexamination by several major banks of their cloud-first AI strategies, with at least three reportedly initiating reviews of on-premise AI infrastructure investments
the_one_insight: The banks that own their inference infrastructure will compound their AI advantage over time. The banks that rent it will always be one pricing change away from losing their edge.

key_quotes.text: Every country, every company needs to own its AI. You wouldn't outsource your electricity grid. You won't outsource your intelligence layer.
key_quotes.context: On the strategic imperative of AI infrastructure ownership

key_quotes.text: We are at the iPhone moment of AI. In five years, the idea of running your financial institution without sovereign AI infrastructure will seem as strange as running without a data center.
key_quotes.context: On the pace and inevitability of AI adoption
